Context documents:

[Document 1]
Title: HR-POL-003: Remote Work Eligibility and Requirements
ID: HR-POL-003-S4.1
Eligible employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week, subject to line manager approval. Core hours of 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM local time, as defined in HR-POL-001 Section 3.2, must be observed on all working days regardless of work location. Remote work eligibility is reviewed quarterly. Roles requiring physical presence or access to specialist equipment are not eligible.

[Document 2]
Title: Quality Manual QM-001 Section 6: IATF 16949 Assembly Line Requirements
ID: ENG-QUAL-IATF-001
IATF 16949 compliance on the assembly line requires: documented control plans for all operations, process FMEAs for all manufacturing steps, measurement system analyses for critical characteristics, and operator work instructions at each workstation in the local language. Internal audits are conducted quarterly by the Quality Systems team. Non-conformances raised during audits must be closed within 30 days.

[Document 3]
Title: Engineering Process: Prototype Build Authorisation
ID: ENG-PROC-PROTO-001
Prototype build authorisation requires a Build Request Form submitted in the PLM system with: programme code, build objectives, bill of materials, derogation list, and budget approval. Sign-off from the Programme Manager and Chief Engineer is required. Build slots are allocated by the Vehicle Programmes team. Standard lead time is 4 weeks from approved request to build slot allocation.

[Document 4]
Title: Procurement Policy: Supplier Performance Review
ID: PROC-001-S6
Supplier performance is reviewed quarterly across four KPIs: on-time delivery rate, parts per million (PPM) defect rate, responsiveness to issues, and commercial compliance. Results are published in the Supplier Portal. Suppliers scoring below threshold for two consecutive quarters are placed on a Supplier Improvement Programme. Sustained underperformance may result in de-listing.

Question: Quick question: How is supplier performance measured and reviewed?

Performance is reviewed quarterly across ##begin_quote##on-time delivery rate, PPM defect rate, responsiveness to issues, and commercial compliance##end_quote##. Two consecutive quarters below threshold triggers a Supplier Improvement Programme.

Context documents:

[Document 1]
Title: Supplier Quality Manual: Engineering Change Requests
ID: PROC-002-S3
Suppliers must submit Engineering Change Requests (ECRs) via the Supplier Portal using the ECR Form before implementing any change to an approved part. The ECR must include the affected part number, description of the proposed change, justification, and supporting test data or drawings. No change may be implemented until written approval is received from our Engineering team. Review takes up to 10 business days.

[Document 2]
Title: Quality Procedure QP-CAL-003: Torque Tool Calibration
ID: ENG-STD-CAL-003
Torque tools used on safety-critical fasteners must be calibrated every 6 months or after any tool drop or suspected damage, whichever occurs first. Calibration must be performed by an approved metrology laboratory. Records are maintained in the Calibration Management System. Tools out of calibration are tagged with an out-of-service label and immediately removed from the production line.

[Document 3]
Title: Procurement Policy: Payment Terms
ID: PROC-001-S3
Standard payment terms are Net 30 days from receipt of a valid approved invoice. Invoices must reference a valid Purchase Order number. Invoices without a PO reference will be rejected. For preferred strategic suppliers, Net 45 or Net 60 terms may apply as specified in the individual Supply Agreement. Early payment discount arrangements are available on request.

[Document 4]
Title: Service Policy: Servicing Schedule
ID: SVC-001-S1
Vehicles should be serviced every 12 months or 10,000 miles, whichever comes first. The service indicator on the instrument cluster will illuminate when a service is due. Adherence to the manufacturer's service schedule is required to maintain warranty validity. Service records must be maintained and are required when making warranty claims.

Question: How often must torque tools be calibrated?

From the context provided: Torque tools on safety-critical fasteners must be calibrated ##begin_quote##every 6 months or after any tool drop or suspected damage, whichever occurs first##end_quote##. Tools out of calibration are ##begin_quote##tagged and immediately removed from the production line##end_quote##.

The vehicle must be within the warranty period and have been serviced at manufacturer-specified intervals using approved parts.

Context documents:

[Document 1]
Title: HR-POL-001: Sick Leave and Mental Health Days
ID: HR-POL-001-S4.2
Employees are entitled to 10 days of paid sick leave per calendar year. Self-certification is accepted for absences of up to 3 consecutive days. A medical certificate from a registered practitioner is required for absences of 4 or more days, submitted within 2 working days of return. In addition, 2 paid Mental Health Days are provided per year, separate from sick leave, and do not require a medical certificate. These are logged under 'Mental Health Leave' in the HRIS portal.

[Document 2]
Title: Service Policy: Roadside Assistance
ID: SVC-001-S2
All new vehicle purchases include 3 years of 24/7 roadside assistance. Coverage includes: breakdown recovery and towing, flat tyre assistance, emergency fuel delivery, battery jump-start, and emergency accommodation if the vehicle cannot be repaired at roadside. Contact roadside assistance at 0800-XXX-XXXX or via the owner mobile app.

[Document 3]
Title: TSB-ENG-2024-047: 2.0T Cylinder Head Bolt Torque Procedure
ID: ENG-TSB-2024-047
Cylinder head bolt torque specifications for the 2.0T turbocharged petrol engine: Stage 1 — 20 Nm, Stage 2 — 60 Nm, Stage 3 — 90° angle tightening. Bolts are torque-to-yield (TTY) and must not be reused — always fit new bolts. Perform coolant purge after assembly per Workshop Manual Section 4.3.2. Failure to follow this procedure may result in head gasket failure and coolant loss.

[Document 4]
Title: HR-POL-002: Disciplinary Procedure
ID: HR-POL-002-S4
The disciplinary procedure consists of: informal discussion, written warning, final written warning, and dismissal. Each stage requires documented evidence and an opportunity for the employee to respond. Employees may be accompanied by a colleague or trade union representative at formal stages. Gross misconduct may result in summary dismissal without prior warning.

Question: Hi, What are the stages of the disciplinary procedure?

The disciplinary procedure consists of ##begin_quote##informal discussion, written warning, final written warning, and dismissal##end_quote##. Each stage requires documented evidence and an opportunity for the employee to respond. Gross misconduct may result in summary dismissal without prior warning.

Title: Quality Manual QM-001 Section 6: IATF 16949 Assembly Line Requirements
ID: ENG-QUAL-IATF-001
IATF 16949 compliance on the assembly line requires: documented control plans for all operations, process FMEAs for all manufacturing steps, measurement system analyses for critical characteristics, and operator work instructions at each workstation in the local language. Internal audits are conducted quarterly by the Quality Systems team. Non-conformances raised during audits must be closed within 30 days.

[Document 2]
Title: Procurement Policy: Supplier Quality Requirements
ID: PROC-001-S5
All component and material suppliers must hold ISO 9001:2015 certification as a minimum. Suppliers to powertrain, safety-critical, and chassis systems divisions must additionally hold IATF 16949 certification. Proof of current, valid certification must be submitted during onboarding and renewed annually. Suppliers found to have lapsed certification will be suspended from the Approved Vendor List pending re-certification.

[Document 3]
Title: TSB-ENG-2024-031: Brake Fluid Specification
ID: ENG-TSB-2024-031
All vehicles in the current model range require DOT 4 brake fluid meeting SAE J1703 and FMVSS 116 standards. DOT grades must not be mixed. Brake fluid should be replaced every 2 years or 30,000 miles as part of the scheduled maintenance programme. Use only fluid from the Approved Parts List. Failure to use the specified fluid may result in brake system degradation and will void the relevant warranty coverage.

Title: Warranty Policy: Standard Warranty Coverage
ID: WTY-001-S1
New vehicles are covered by a 3-year or 60,000-mile manufacturer's warranty (whichever comes first) covering defects in materials and workmanship. The powertrain warranty extends to 5 years or 100,000 miles. Warranty service must be carried out at an authorised dealer. Consumables including tyres, brake pads, clutch components, and wiper blades are excluded from warranty coverage.

[Document 2]
Title: TSB-ENG-2024-047: 2.0T Cylinder Head Bolt Torque Procedure
ID: ENG-TSB-2024-047
Cylinder head bolt torque specifications for the 2.0T turbocharged petrol engine: Stage 1 — 20 Nm, Stage 2 — 60 Nm, Stage 3 — 90° angle tightening. Bolts are torque-to-yield (TTY) and must not be reused — always fit new bolts. Perform coolant purge after assembly per Workshop Manual Section 4.3.2. Failure to follow this procedure may result in head gasket failure and coolant loss.

[Document 3]
Title: Weld Specification WS-CHASSIS-002: Structural Chassis Repairs
ID: ENG-STD-WELD-002
Chassis structural welds must use MIG welding with ER70S-6 wire. Pre-heat requirements and minimum interpass temperatures are specified per material grade in Table 3 of this document. Post-weld inspection must be carried out by a Level 2 NDT technician before sign-off. Only welders holding current BS EN ISO 9606-1 qualification are authorised to perform structural chassis repairs.

[Document 4]
Title: Procurement Policy: Purchase Order Process
ID: PROC-001-S4
Purchase Orders are raised by the Procurement team following internal approval of a Purchase Requisition. POs are transmitted electronically via the Supplier Portal or email. Suppliers must acknowledge PO receipt within 2 business days. No goods or services should be supplied without a valid, current PO number. Verbal or email-only authorisation is not valid.

Question: Hello — What welding process is approved for chassis structural repairs?

From the context provided: Structural chassis repairs must use ##begin_quote##MIG welding with ER70S-6 wire##end_quote##. Post-weld inspection must be carried out by ##begin_quote##a Level 2 NDT technician before sign-off##end_quote##. Only welders with current BS EN ISO 9606-1 qualification may perform this work.

Gross misconduct may result in summary dismissal without prior warning.

[Document 4]
Title: HR-POL-001: Parental Leave
ID: HR-POL-001-S4.3
Primary caregivers are entitled to 16 weeks of paid parental leave. Secondary caregivers are entitled to 4 weeks of paid parental leave. Leave may commence up to 4 weeks before the expected due date for primary caregivers. Employees must notify HR at least 8 weeks before the intended start date. Parental leave does not affect annual leave accrual.

ID: ENG-SPEC-FUEL-004

Any failure must be escalated immediately to the Fuel Systems Lead before the vehicle is cleared for further testing or delivery.

Tools out of calibration are tagged with an out-of-service label and immediately removed from the production line.

[Document 2]
Title: TSB-ENG-2024-047: 2.0T Cylinder Head Bolt Torque Procedure
ID: ENG-TSB-2024-047
Cylinder head bolt torque specifications for the 2.0T turbocharged petrol engine: Stage 1 — 20 Nm, Stage 2 — 60 Nm, Stage 3 — 90° angle tightening. Bolts are torque-to-yield (TTY) and must not be reused — always fit new bolts. Perform coolant purge after assembly per Workshop Manual Section 4.3.2. Failure to follow this procedure may result in head gasket failure and coolant loss.

[Document 3]
Title: Procurement Policy: Supplier Performance Review
ID: PROC-001-S6
Supplier performance is reviewed quarterly across four KPIs: on-time delivery rate, parts per million (PPM) defect rate, responsiveness to issues, and commercial compliance. Sustained underperformance may result in de-listing.

[Document 4]
Title: Weld Specification WS-CHASSIS-002: Structural Chassis Repairs
ID: ENG-STD-WELD-002
Chassis structural welds must use MIG welding with ER70S-6 wire. Pre-heat requirements and minimum interpass temperatures are specified per material grade in Table 3 of this document. Post-weld inspection must be carried out by a Level 2 NDT technician before sign-off. Only welders holding current BS EN ISO 9606-1 qualification are authorised to perform structural chassis repairs.

ID: ENG-TSB-2024-047

Title: HR-POL-001: Sick Leave and Mental Health Days
ID: HR-POL-001-S4.2
Employees are entitled to 10 days of paid sick leave per calendar year. Self-certification is accepted for absences of up to 3 consecutive days. A medical certificate from a registered practitioner is required for absences of 4 or more days, submitted within 2 working days of return. In addition, 2 paid Mental Health Days are provided per year, separate from sick leave, and do not require a medical certificate. These are logged under 'Mental Health Leave' in the HRIS portal.

Title: Procurement Policy: Purchase Order Process
ID: PROC-001-S4
Purchase Orders are raised by the Procurement team following internal approval of a Purchase Requisition. POs are transmitted electronically via the Supplier Portal or email. Suppliers must acknowledge PO receipt within 2 business days. No goods or services should be supplied without a valid, current PO number. Verbal or email-only authorisation is not valid.

[Document 3]
Title: Service Policy: Connected Services and OTA Updates
ID: SVC-001-S4
Over-the-air (OTA) software updates are delivered automatically when the vehicle is connected to Wi-Fi. A notification will appear on the infotainment screen when an update is available. Updates can be scheduled for a time when the vehicle is parked. Connected services, including remote locking and vehicle health reports, are activated via the owner app at owners.company.com.

Title: Quality Procedure QP-CAL-003: Torque Tool Calibration
ID: ENG-STD-CAL-003
Torque tools used on safety-critical fasteners must be calibrated every 6 months or after any tool drop or suspected damage, whichever occurs first. Calibration must be performed by an approved metrology laboratory. Records are maintained in the Calibration Management System. Tools out of calibration are tagged with an out-of-service label and immediately removed from the production line.
